full_text:
The sample was one big and hard tumor piece that was cut into halves. Used half and gave the rest to Kauppi lab.

The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated using Dispase, followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H).

Dispase dissociation media included 19.4 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12 supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 400 µl Dispase + 200 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37C rotating for 30 min, vortexed before and after. Filtered through 70 µm cell strainer.

Further dissociated the leftover tissue pieces with 17.8 ml DMEM/F12+++, 2 ml Collagenase & Hyaluronidase, and 200 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37C rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

RBC lysis with 5 ml for 5 min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++ for both conditions.

Dispase dissociated cells: After centrifugation, the cell pellet still had some red, so the RBC lysis was redone with 2 ml for 5 min + 10 ml.

Dispase dissociated cells: The final pellet was resuspended in 5 ml of iPDC media.

Sequentially dissociated cells: The final pellet was resuspended in 4 of DMEM/F12+++.

For counting, a 1:10 dilution of both cell suspensions was made.

Cell counts:

Made 1 aliquot with 1 E6 cells from both dispase and sequentially dissociated cells.

Took the rest (~180 E6 cells) of sequential dissociated cells for enrichment.

Biobanking

Dispase dissociated cells: 4x 5E6 cells

Sequentially dissociated, negative isolation flowthrough: 2x 1.2 E6 cells

Labelled: S540_pOme2 1.2 E6 cells D+C&H Fib + End 27.03.2025 AE/MH

Cells were frozen in 1mL of STEM-CellBanker.

Magnetic-activated cell sorting (MACS)

Performed negative isolation for all obtained sequentially dissociatated cells. Cell count was 183.8 E6, and divided that into 3 equal fractions. Made the calculations assuming 60 E6 cells in 3 separate fractions.

Negative isolation: Depleting the sample from cancer and immune cells using a mixture of anti-EpCAM (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-061-101) and anti-CD45 (TIL) beads (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-118-780), and an LS column in a QuadroMACS separator.

CD326 (EpCAM) MicroBeads, and CD45 MicroBeads protocols by Miltenyi Biotec were used as guidance.

MACS buffer was prepared as follows: 49.55 ml DPBS (w/o MgCl2 and CaCl2) + 250 µl FBS + 200 µl EDTA to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM EDTA in DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and sterile-filtered before being used.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice.

Everything was done inside a laminar hood to keep the cells sterile.

Centrifuged the cells at 300 g for 10 min at +4C.

The pellets were resuspended in 260 µl of cold MACS buffer (calculation: the desired volume [500 µl] - the volume of both beads [2x 120 µl]). Added 120 µl of both anti-EpCAM and anti-CD45 beads to the cell suspension and incubated 30 min at +4C shielded from light.

Washed the cells with 2 ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 1500 rpm (408 g) for 5 min at +4C. Lastly, the cells were resuspended in 500 µl of MACS buffer.

Placed three LS columns and 30 µm filters in the magnetic MACS Separator. Prepared them by washing them with 3 ml of MACS buffer, then applied the cell suspension onto the column, and washed with 3 x 3 ml MACS buffer.

Columns were removed from the separator and placed on a new 15 ml falcon tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was added to the column and with firmly pushing the plunger into the column, magnetically labeled cells were flushed out.

All cell suspensions were centrifuged 1500 rpm g for 5 min. Resuspended the flow-throught cells in 2 ml iPDC media and the column-bound fracton in 4 ml DMEM/F12+++ media. Counted the cells.

Took 0.8 E6 live cells from the flowthrough fraction for flow cytometry, and 2x 0.8 E6 live cells (~1.2 E6 cells) for biobanking.

Combining dispase dissociated cells and cells from the flowthrough fraction

Combined 4.45 E6 live cells from negative isolation flow though fraction with 4.45 E6 live cells of dispase dissociated cells.

Cell count of combined cells was 6.15 E6 cells/ml in ~1.3 mL → in total 8.9 E6 cells

Took 6x 0.78 E6 cells for flow cytometry: 1x multicolor, and 5x FMOs.

With these calculations, there would be 4.16 E6 cells for plating.

Cell culture

1 000 live cells/uL * 40 uL = 40 000 cells/droplet

40 000 cells/droplet * 8 droplets/well * 13 wells (one extra) = 4 160 000 live cells are needed

8 droplets/ well * 13 wells * 40 uL/ droplet = 4 160 uL are needed in total

However, since the omentum gel starts polymerizing as soon as the fibrinogen is added to the mixture, it is better to make eight (8) separate tubes each one of 520 uL (4160 uL/8) and not everything at once in a single tube.

We want to keep a cell density of 1 000 live cells/uL, so the total number of cells per tube that will be needed is:

1 000 live cells/uL * 520 uL = 0.52 E6 live cells/tube

0.52 E6 cells / (6.15 E6 cells/mL) = 84.55 uL from the original cell suspension

The amount of the remaining components per tube will be:

ORG full medium: (520 uL – 213.1 uL) = 306.9 uL

Omentum gel: 115 uL

Aprotinin: 1.56

Thrombin: 1.56

Fibrinogen: 10.4

Total (ome-gel+apro+thro+fib) = 128.5 uL

Total (+cells) = 213.1 uL

Note: When preparing the tubes, the cell suspension was not enough and, because of this, only 6 tubes were prepared and 6 wells seeded as follows:

The droplets were allowed to gelify for 1 hour in the incubator and, after that time, 3.5 mL of iPDC media + cytokines were added into each well. Pictures were taken in the cell culture microscope under all the magnifications available.

Flow cytometry

The conditions to stain were:

DCM FMO

CD45 FMO

EpCAM FMO

CD8 FMO

CD3 FMO

CD31 FMO

FAP FMO

dispase dissociated cells, MC

sequentially dissociated cells/unpurified, MC

negative isolation flowthrough, MC

negative isolation column bound, MC

combined cells, MC

The aliquots were stained with the following panel.

Note: The FAP dilution was changed from 1 in 25 to 1 in 50, as this marker was unnecessarily too bright.

Washed cells once with 900 µl FACS buffer (centrifuge 6min 400 g at +4°C).

The antibodies were diluted in Brilliant stain buffer (#563794 BD Horizon),, 50 µl of the antibody mix was added to each sample, and the samples were incubated for 30 min at +4C shielded from light.

For the FMO samples, antibody mixes were prepared separately

After incubation, washed once with 900 µl FACS buffer.

Added 200 µl IC Fixation buffer (#00-8222 Invitrogen) to fix the cells and incubated for 40 min at +4°C shielded from light.

After incubation, washed once with 900 µl FACS buffer.

Resuspended in 900 µl FACS buffer for overnight storage.

Next morning, centrifuged and resuspended the cells in 400 µl FACS buffer.

Pipeted the cell suspension through a 5 ml Falcon polystyrene tube with a cell-strainer cap (blue).

Preparation of compensation controls

Used BD CompBeads Plus (7.5 µm) compensation beads.

Added one drop of both positive (BD CompBeads AntiMouse IgG, #51-9006274) and negative (CompBeads Negative Control #51-9006227) compensation beads to 5ml Falcon polystyrene round bottom tubes (white cap).

For stained compensation controls, 2 µl of each antibody was used.

the same antibodies except CD16-BV510 instead of BV510-DCM.

Incubated for 30 min at +4°C shielded from light and resuspended to 350 µl FACS buffer.

After this staining, the CD31 antibody (Clone WM59, Cat# 303117, Lot# B402402) vial was used up.
